survival_criteria.pass_condition: family patch separation remains above current large-inventory floor under scaffolded readout intervention
survival_criteria.fail_condition: cross-family mixing rises to same-family band under scaffolded readout

survival_criteria.pass_condition: reasoning-slice transport continues to beat readout-only contrast while preserving restricted overlap behavior
survival_criteria.fail_condition: reasoning transport gain disappears or only survives under direct collapse

survival_criteria.pass_condition: stage-conditioned transport exceeds stage-free readout under long-chain constraints
survival_criteria.fail_condition: stage labels do not improve transport once family and reasoning slice are controlled

survival_criteria.pass_condition: successor-aware transport preserves chain-local successor coherence better than chain-agnostic transport
survival_criteria.fail_condition: successor-aware transport yields no coherence gain over chain-agnostic transport

survival_criteria.pass_condition: write narrows before read collapse under stress while stage-aware admissibility remains valid
survival_criteria.fail_condition: stress directly destroys readout without guarded-write asymmetry

survival_criteria.pass_condition: relation lift remains family-anchored while causal successor structure is preserved
survival_criteria.fail_condition: relation lift only survives after losing family anchoring or successor coherence

verdict.core_answer: Long-chain inventory now supports an explicit six-theorem survival framework, with two ready now, two next under stage/chain-aware interventions, and two queued behind later dynamic closures.
verdict.next_theory_target: promote stage-conditioned transport and successor alignment into the next intervention priority block.
